inline_comments.message: hmm.  Don't think my VM has a "/maven", so this won't work there, will it?
I don't think "bash" is needed, as long as it's specified at the top of the .sh script.
never mind this comment.  Appears to work in xacml, so I'll assume it will work here, too.  (Obviously, more I need to learn about docker builds.)
Looks like it according to: https://jira.onap.org/browse/POLICY-1808

the docker-maven-plugin by default outputs the artifacts defined by dependencySet config into target/<image-name_in_plugin_config>/build/maven root. I could specify an outputDirectory but it would create a subpath in maven dir
oh, duh, that's the whole point of this - thx!
Any reason to change if from $HOME ?   $HOME is more portable, cause user account changes.

doesn't $HOME depend on "docker build" being run as "policy", whereas this should work regardless of who runs it?

On the other hand, will the "unzip" command work, or does it need to specify a path for the zip file?  Or maybe do this first?

  WORKDIR ${POLICY_INSTALL}

For that matter, why bother copying the zip file at all?

"cause" -> "case" typo above, not sure caused you confusion, rephrasing comment:  "$HOME is more portable, in case user account for some reason is changed in in parent image"  Functionally this will work, but don't see need to change it.

yes, unzip is necessary as is, it has been working for a while (same as $HOME).

1. If I do not specify the /home/policy the "docker build" resolves $HOME to my host home directory.
```[ERROR] Failed to execute goal io.fabric8:docker-maven-plugin:0.30.0:build (generate-images) on project docker-controlloop: Unable to build image [onap/policy-pdpd-cl] : "The command '/bin/sh -c unzip apps-controlloop.zip &&     mkdir -p ${POLICY_HOME}/logs ${POLICY_HOME}/config /Users/rpujar/.m2 &&     tar -C ${POLICY_HOME} -xvf base-${BUILD_VERSION_DROOLS}.tar.gz --no-same-owner &&     tar -C ${POLICY_HOME} -xvf basex-controlloop-${BUILD_VERSION_APP_CL}.tar.gz --no-same-owner &&     unzip policy-management-${BUILD_VERSION_DROOLS}.zip -d ${POLICY_HOME} &&     echo \"source ${POLICY_HOME}/etc/profile.d/env.sh\" >> \"/Users/rpujar/.bashrc\" &&     chmod 700 ${POLICY_HOME}/bin/* &&     chmod 600 ${POLICY_HOME}/config/* &&     chmod 700 ${POLICY_INSTALL}/*.sh &&     rm -f ${POLICY_INSTALL}/*.conf' returned a non-zero code: 1"  -> [Help 1]```

2. Without including bash in entrypoint "ENTRYPOINT ["bash", "/opt/app/policy/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh"]" the container fails to startup with permissions issue on the docker-entrypoint.sh script.
```$ docker run -it onap/policy-pdpd-cl:latest bash
docker: Error response from daemon: OCI runtime create failed: container_linux.go:344: starting container process caused "exec: \"/opt/app/policy/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh\": permission denied": unknown.```

However, changing the permissions on the shell script returns an error:
```[ERROR] DOCKER> Unable to build image [onap/policy-pdpd-cl] : "The command '/bin/sh -c chmod 700 ${POLICY_HOME}/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh' returned a non-zero code: 1"  ["The command '/bin/sh -c chmod 700 ${POLICY_HOME}/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh' returned a non-zero code: 1" ]```

3. Also shouldn't the drools-application docker use onap/policy-drools:1.5.0-SNAPSHOT as the base image?

It is rather weird, this happens to me even when I was building docker images old way.

regarding #3, can't simply move the "chmod 700" from line 14 to before line 22?

Is your drools-applications directory on your local VM?  Or is it a Windows directory that's shared with your VM?  I know the latter can cause permission issues.  Also, does group/other have read & execute permissions on all of the files & directories?  If not, that can also cause permission issues.  I thought both of these permission issues I mention in this paragraph only apply when using volume mounts within docker, but maybe they apply to the docker build step, too?

Anyway, you got it working the other way, so let's just leave it that way.
For later, we need to be able to configure this so production uses release version vs development uses SNAPSHOT.
